2606.16605.title: ARB4WM: An Adversarial Robustness Benchmark for World Models in Continuous Control
2606.16605.paper_summary: World models are widely used in robotic and agentic engineering control systems due to their ability to learn latent dynamics for planning and decision-making. As these systems are increasingly deployed in safety-critical settings, understanding their robustness under adversarial conditions has become essential. However, existing evaluations lack a unified benchmark for testing adversarial threats across the policy, value, and latent-dynamics levels of world-model agents. To fill this gap, we present ARB4WM, a unified evaluation framework for pre-deployment robustness and risk assessment of world-model agents under visual perturbations. ARB4WM defines five white-box loss objectives across these three levels and studies their effects when combined with single-step or multi-step perturbation strategies and temporal attack modes, including full-frame, half-sequence, and sparse-frame exposure. Specifically, we evaluate four Dreamer-style agents across 20 tasks from MetaWorld and the DeepMind Control Suite under different loss objectives, perturbation strategies, and temporal attack modes. Results show that attacks targeting value estimation, latent representations, and RSSM dynamics can be as damaging as direct policy disruption, and that early or frequent perturbations are especially harmful, while input-level defenses provide limited recovery under adaptive attacks. These findings suggest that safety, risk, and reliability assessment for world models should cover multiple component-oriented attack objectives and temporal exposure protocols rather than relying solely on action-space robustness. Source code is available at https://github.com/zaoanguai/ARB4WM.
2606.16605.paper_summary_zh: 世界模型因其能學習潛在動態以進行規劃和決策而廣泛應用於機器人和代理工程控制系統中。隨著這些系統在安全關鍵環境中的部署越來越多，了解它們在對抗條件下的穩健性變得至關重要。然而，現有的評估缺乏統一的基準來測試世界模型代理在政策、價值和潛在動態層面的對抗威脅。為了填補這一空白，我們提出了ARB4WM，一個統一的評估框架，用於在視覺擾動下進行世界模型代理的預部署穩健性和風險評估。ARB4WM在這三個層面上定義了五個白盒損失目標，並研究了它們在與單步或多步擾動策略及時間攻擊模式（包括全幀、半序列和稀疏幀暴露）結合時的效果。具體而言，我們在不同的損失目標、擾動策略和時間攻擊模式下，對來自MetaWorld和DeepMind Control Suite的20個任務進行了四個Dreamer風格代理的評估。結果顯示，針對價值估計、潛在表示和RSSM動態的攻擊可能與直接政策擾動一樣具破壞性，而早期或頻繁的擾動尤其有害，而輸入層級的防禦在自適應攻擊下提供的恢復有限。這些發現表明，對世界模型的安全性、風險和可靠性評估應涵蓋多個以組件為導向的攻擊目標和時間暴露協議，而不僅僅依賴於行動空間的穩健性。源代碼可在 https://github.com/zaoanguai/ARB4WM 獲得。
2606.16605.author: Junjian Zhang et.al.
2606.16605.authors: Junjian Zhang, Hao Tan, Ruonan Li, Dong Zhu, Aiping Li, Zhaoquan Gu
